---
title: "Figure 5 (macro_IR information vs interval & channels) — the experimental-design trade-off"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
How the per-recording parameter uncertainty (macro_IR, honest since distortion ~ 1) depends on the two
design knobs: measurement interval and channel number. Component = Likelihood_Fisher_Covariance diagonal
(variance of each parameter estimate); SE = sqrt(var), in dex. Reading the two incremental questions:
  - doubling the interval: how much does the SE grow going Delta -> 2*Delta (the slope in log-log).
  - N twice vs 2N once: info(2N)/[2*info(N)] = var(N)/(2*var(2N)); >1 means concentrating channels beats
    spreading them (super-linear info in N, expected when instrument noise dominates at few channels).
macro_IR, noise 0.1, data 433ed13, all params, all N_ch. Log-log, 95% bootstrap CI ribbons.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
COV      <- "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"   # gaussian run (1c2ae6f); Likelihood_ is empty there
PIDX     <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB<- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
              unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

d <- map_dfr(Sys.glob("../data/1c2ae6f/figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_0.1_battery_pool_G.csv"),
             ~ read.csv(.x, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))

dd <- d %>%
  filter(component_path == COV, statistic == "value", param_index == param_col,
         param_index %in% PIDX,
         probit == "mean" | (probit == "quantile" & quantile_level %in% c(0.025, 0.975))) %>%
  mutate(param_name = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)],
         q = if_else(probit == "mean", "v", if_else(quantile_level == 0.025, "lo", "hi"))) %>%
  group_by(Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_name, q) %>%
  summarise(x = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = q, values_from = x) %>%
  mutate(SE = sqrt(v), SE_lo = sqrt(pmax(lo, 0)), SE_hi = sqrt(hi),
         nch_f = factor(Num_ch),
         param = factor(param_name, levels = names(PIDX), labels = unname(PARAM_LAB)))
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.5, fig.height = 5.6}
g <- ggplot(dd, aes(interval_in_tau, SE, colour = nch_f, fill = nch_f, group = nch_f)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = SE_lo, ymax = SE_hi), alpha = .12, colour = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .5) + geom_point(size = .9) +
  facet_wrap(~ param, nrow = 2, labeller = label_parsed, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(.01, .1, 1)) + scale_y_log10() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(name = expression(N[ch]), end = .92, direction = -1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = expression(N[ch]), end = .92, direction = -1) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]),
       y = "per-recording SE of estimate (dex), 95% CI") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7.5), axis.title = element_text(size = 8.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8.5))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_IR_covariance_design.pdf", g, width = 7.5, height = 5.6)
```

```{r incremental}
# (1) doubling the interval: variance ratio var(2Delta)/var(Delta) at matched N_ch (nearest 2x pairs)
pair2 <- function(x) { s <- sort(unique(x)); tibble(a = s[-length(s)], b = s[-1]) }
cat("=== (1) info lost doubling the interval: var(next)/var(this), median over N_ch, per param ===\n")
d1 <- dd %>% select(Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_name, v) %>%
  group_by(Num_ch, param_name) %>% arrange(interval_in_tau) %>%
  mutate(ratio_var = lead(v) / v, dlog = log10(lead(interval_in_tau) / interval_in_tau)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ratio_var))
print(d1 %>% group_by(param_name) %>%
        summarise(median_var_growth_per_step = round(median(ratio_var), 2), .groups = "drop") %>% as.data.frame())

# (2) N twice vs 2N once: info(2N)/[2 info(N)] = var(N)/(2 var(2N)) for exact 2x N_ch pairs
cat("\n=== (2) concentrate vs spread: var(N)/(2*var(2N)); >1 => 2N-once beats N-twice ===\n")
pairsN <- tibble(N = c(10, 50, 100), N2 = c(20, 100, 200))   # exact 2x N_ch pairs present in 1c2ae6f
d2 <- dd %>% select(Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_name, v) %>%
  inner_join(pairsN, by = c("Num_ch" = "N")) %>%
  inner_join(dd %>% select(Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_name, v2 = v),
             by = c("N2" = "Num_ch", "interval_in_tau", "param_name")) %>%
  mutate(concentrate = v / (2 * v2),
         reg = if_else(interval_in_tau <= 0.05, "fine", if_else(interval_in_tau >= 0.5, "coarse", "mid")))
print(d2 %>% group_by(param_name) %>%
        summarise(overall = round(median(concentrate), 2), .groups = "drop") %>% as.data.frame())
print(d2 %>% filter(reg != "mid") %>% group_by(param_name, reg) %>%
        summarise(c = round(median(concentrate), 2), .groups = "drop") %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = reg, values_from = c) %>% as.data.frame())
```
